dismissalText.commentary: Priyansh Arya c Parakh b Tiwari 56 (33b 2x4 6x6 54m) SR: 169.69

dismissalComment.html: skewed up, and taken! Tiwari with his maiden IPL wicket, and he has sent back the set batter! Fullish in the channel and this seems to move away with the angle. Arya looks to go inside-out and to be fair, it is there to be hit. Slices across it a little too much, though, and Parakh accepts the offering with glee at deep point. Arya walks back after an initially tone-setting knock, and DC keep chipping away!

dismissalText.long: c Auqib Nabi b Mukesh Kumar
dismissalText.commentary: Prabhsimran Singh c Auqib Nabi b Mukesh Kumar 18 (15b 3x4 0x6 42m) SR: 120

dismissalComment.html: shanked to deep backward square leg, and Prabhsimran's troubles come to an end! Shortish and tucking up the batter on middle and leg. Prabh looks for the pick-up pull but has no room to work with. Miscues it and Nabi, diving forward on the fence, does the rest. DC on the board, finally, and it is just reward for the squeeze they have applied!

dismissalText.commentary: Cooper Connolly c Miller b Tiwari 38 (27b 3x4 2x6 46m) SR: 140.74

dismissalComment.html: an attempted tennis forehand, and it leads to Connolly's downfall! Slower bumper outside off and this takes an eternity to arrive. Connolly plays three strokes in his head before making contact with his fourth and actual attempt, which is something resembling a top-spin forehand in tennis. Does not get enough distance on it and Miller does the rest at deep mid wicket!

dismissalText.commentary: Marcus Stoinis c Tiwari b Starc 1 (2b 0x4 0x6 3m) SR: 50

dismissalComment.html: slapped straight to extra cover, and Stoinis has to go! Shortish outside off and this seems to just hold in the surface. Stoinis looks to go over the fielder but does not muster the elevation he wants. Tame chip to Tiwari in the end, and Starc, too, has a wicket now!

dismissalText.commentary: Shashank Singh c Patel b Starc 0 (1b 0x4 0x6 1m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: two wickets in two balls, and Starc, all of a sudden, is on a hat-trick now! Full outside off. Shashank plants his front foot and swings through the line. Slices it, though, and Axar completes the catch at mid off with minimal fuss. PBKS losing their way a little in this over!

dismissalText.long: c Auqib Nabi b Mukesh Kumar
dismissalText.commentary: Prabhsimran Singh c Auqib Nabi b Mukesh Kumar 18 (15b 3x4 0x6 42m) SR: 120

dismissalComment.html: shanked to deep backward square leg, and Prabhsimran's troubles come to an end! Shortish and tucking up the batter on middle and leg. Prabh looks for the pick-up pull but has no room to work with. Miscues it and Nabi, diving forward on the fence, does the rest. DC on the board, finally, and it is just reward for the squeeze they have applied!

dismissalText.commentary: Priyansh Arya c Parakh b Tiwari 56 (33b 2x4 6x6 54m) SR: 169.69

dismissalComment.html: skewed up, and taken! Tiwari with his maiden IPL wicket, and he has sent back the set batter! Fullish in the channel and this seems to move away with the angle. Arya looks to go inside-out and to be fair, it is there to be hit. Slices across it a little too much, though, and Parakh accepts the offering with glee at deep point. Arya walks back after an initially tone-setting knock, and DC keep chipping away!

dismissalComment.html: an attempted tennis forehand, and it leads to Connolly's downfall! Slower bumper outside off and this takes an eternity to arrive. Connolly plays three strokes in his head before making contact with his fourth and actual attempt, which is something resembling a top-spin forehand in tennis. Does not get enough distance on it and Miller does the rest at deep mid wicket!

dismissalText.commentary: KL Rahul c Jansen b Arshdeep Singh 9 (8b 2x4 0x6 14m) SR: 112.5

dismissalComment.html: goes straight up, and Jansen snares the chance! Hard length on middle and leg, and this stands up a touch. Rahul looks to swivel and help it on its way but the ball skews off the top edge. Prabhsimran runs back, as does Jansen, but Jansen calls loudly enough in the end to overtake Prabhsiram and take the catch diving low to his left. Top grab, and DC in trouble early!

dismissalText.commentary: Abishek Porel b Yash Thakur 5 (6b 1x4 0x6 9m) SR: 83.33

dismissalComment.html: middle stump out of the ground, and the change in angle has worked a charm! Slanted in on a length in the channel, bowled at 143.kph. Jags back in with the angle and Porel, looking to drive on the up, is all at sea. Misses it completely and sees his middle stump in a mess soon after. First over this season for Yash but he has not missed a beat!

Arrows it to short third, where Chahal lunges forward and gobbles it up, despite a slight bobble. DC three down inside five overs!

dismissalText.commentary: Tristan Stubbs run out (Connolly) 12 (17b 1x4 0x6 30m) SR: 70.58

dismissalComment.html: huge mix-up, and Stubbs has been run-out! Axar distraught, and the Punjab Kings are delighted! Back of a length on middle and off. Axar seems to jam it off the inside edge, with the ball then rolling towards Connolly at backward point. Stubbs hares down, only to be sent back by his skipper, who, by the way, did not move a muscle. Connolly's throw is right on point and Stubbs, despite a dive, is caught well short. PBKS with a timely breakthrough!

dismissalText.commentary: David Miller c †Prabhsimran Singh b Dwarshuis 51 (28b 3x4 4x6 42m) SR: 182.14

dismissalComment.html: oh my - this has gone so far up. Comes down with rain on it, and Prabhsimran lunges forward to snaffle it up! Hard length just outside off. Miller opens up his stance and swipes across the line. Gets a massive leading edge and the ball swirls for an eternity. Prabhsimran calls early, runs forward and then dives to complete the catch in the short extra cover region. Miller is livid with himself, but this is a massive moment in the game!

dismissalText.long: c sub (P Dubey) b Yash Thakur
dismissalText.commentary: Ashutosh Sharma c sub (P Dubey) b Yash Thakur 24 (10b 2x4 2x6 27m) SR: 240

dismissalComment.html: in the air, in the air, and taken! And this game turns yet again! This is banged half-way down and wide outside off. Ashutosh tries to fight the line this time rather than using it, and pays the price. Tries to fetch it and skies his swat. Dubey, on as a sub, runs to his right from long on and snares it. Ashutosh not happy with himself. His cameo has put DC on the verge of a win, but someone else will have to take them past the finish line!

Angling into leg stump too, meaning Parakh is tucked up completely. Tries to dab it away but cannot keep it down. Arrows it to short third, where Chahal lunges forward and gobbles it up, despite a slight bobble. DC three down inside five overs!

dismissalText.commentary: Tristan Stubbs run out (Connolly) 12 (17b 1x4 0x6 30m) SR: 70.58

dismissalComment.html: huge mix-up, and Stubbs has been run-out! Axar distraught, and the Punjab Kings are delighted! Back of a length on middle and off. Axar seems to jam it off the inside edge, with the ball then rolling towards Connolly at backward point. Stubbs hares down, only to be sent back by his skipper, who, by the way, did not move a muscle. Connolly's throw is right on point and Stubbs, despite a dive, is caught well short. PBKS with a timely breakthrough!

dismissalText.commentary: David Miller c †Prabhsimran Singh b Dwarshuis 51 (28b 3x4 4x6 42m) SR: 182.14

dismissalComment.html: oh my - this has gone so far up. Comes down with rain on it, and Prabhsimran lunges forward to snaffle it up! Hard length just outside off. Miller opens up his stance and swipes across the line. Gets a massive leading edge and the ball swirls for an eternity. Prabhsimran calls early, runs forward and then dives to complete the catch in the short extra cover region. Miller is livid with himself, but this is a massive moment in the game!

dismissalText.long: c sub (P Dubey) b Yash Thakur
dismissalText.commentary: Ashutosh Sharma c sub (P Dubey) b Yash Thakur 24 (10b 2x4 2x6 27m) SR: 240

dismissalComment.html: in the air, in the air, and taken! And this game turns yet again! This is banged half-way down and wide outside off. Ashutosh tries to fight the line this time rather than using it, and pays the price. Tries to fetch it and skies his swat. Dubey, on as a sub, runs to his right from long on and snares it. Ashutosh not happy with himself. His cameo has put DC on the verge of a win, but someone else will have to take them past the finish line!
